ops.title: spawnllm on the cc-pool machine — isolated spawns can't reach Keychain auth; extract a pool token into CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN; enrich routes to codex by 'review' specialty

ops.body:
**Isolated-spawn auth (the operational gotcha as of 2026-07-03, supersedes the PATH
one).** spawnllm runs every call with `isolated=True` (not exposed via `extract()`):
a fresh temp `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` seeded ONLY from `~/.claude/.claude.json` +
`~/.claude/.credentials.json`. On this machine that breaks in two ways:

1. Claude Code's macOS Keychain service name is hashed from the config dir
   (`Claude Code-credentials-<hash>`; plain `~/.claude` uses the unhashed name), so
   an isolated temp dir can NEVER match a Keychain item.
2. `~/.claude/.credentials.json` is a stale June-10 relic (expired token → every
   spawned call exits 1 with `Failed to authenticate ... 401` **on stdout**, stderr
   empty; `run_verdicts` swallows it → "judged 0 rows (N failed)" with zero
   diagnostics). Do NOT overwrite it from the Keychain: the Keychain payload has
   `expiresAt: 0`, which the file loader reads as not-logged-in, and reusing the
   personal refreshToken outside the Keychain risks rotation logging the user out.

**Working fix:** extract a cc-pool account's access token and pass it via env —
`CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` overrides config-dir auth in the spawned CLI:

```sh
export CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN=$(python3 -c "
import json, subprocess
s = subprocess.run(['security','find-generic-password','-s','Claude Code-credentials-<hash>','-w'], capture_output=True, text=True, check=True)
print(json.loads(s.stdout.strip())['claudeAiOauth']['accessToken'], end='')")
uv run cc-pushback triage --model large ...
```

Pick the account via `ccp env` (defaults to emptiest; `ccp list` maps acct → Keychain
service; avoid the account running the current session). Pool access tokens expire in
~hours — re-extract per run; failed rows stay pending and retry (incremental by
design). Verified end-to-end 2026-07-03 (352-row v5 pass).

**Judge model tier:** the v5 verdict corpus is keyed `model=opus` = `--model large`
(`medium`→sonnet would double-judge everything under a different model key).

**Backend routing differs by stage.** The judge/refine call `default_backend()` =
`select_backend()` with no specialty → **claude** (general). But enrich's
correction-picker calls `usable_backend()` = `select_backend(specialty="review")`,
which the registry routes to **codex** (`LLM_BACKENDS = {debugging: codex, review:
codex, general: claude}`). So enrich needs `codex` separately authed (`codex login`);
`codex login status` can say "Logged in using ChatGPT" while the refresh token is
revoked (real calls 401). Verify with a one-row extraction, not the status command.

**PATH (older gotcha, still applies in bare non-interactive shells):** `claude` must
resolve or `select_backend()` silently falls back to codex → empty verdicts. This
machine resolves via `~/.superset/bin/claude`; elsewhere prefix
`PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"`.

**Structured output is a single result-envelope.** The CLI emits one
`{"type":"result", ..., "structured_output":{...}}` object and the backend's
`parse_response(stdout, model)` validates it. The claude backend is stdout-based; the
codex backend (`codex exec`) writes its result to a `-o` file (mismatch fixed in the
spawnllm-0.5 / cc-transcript-7 migration, cc-pushback 0.7.0).

Upstream fix worth filing on spawnllm: `_isolated_dir()` should honor an ambient
`CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` when seeding (and can't fully work with Keychain-only accounts —
env-token injection is the portable answer). See [[cc-pushback-two-stage-pipeline]].
